description: Iraq invades Kuwait, triggering the Gulf War and a sharp oil supply shock.
Currency and financial crisis across Asia sharply reduces regional oil demand.
Terrorist attacks in the US create global economic uncertainty affecting energy markets.
US-led invasion of Iraq disrupts Middle East oil production and raises risk premiums.
Brent crude reaches an all-time high amid strong demand and speculative activity.
Collapse of Lehman Brothers triggers a global recession and demand collapse, crashing oil prices.
Unrest across the Middle East and Libyan civil war disrupt oil supply from North Africa.
OPEC declines to cut output despite a growing supply glut, contributing to a price collapse.

event_name: OPEC Decision Not to Cut Production
description: OPEC formally maintains production quotas, accelerating the 2014-2016 price crash.
OPEC agrees to its first production cut in 8 years to stabilize falling prices.

event_name: US Withdraws from Iran Nuclear Deal
description: US reinstates sanctions on Iran, reducing Iranian oil exports and tightening supply.
Attacks temporarily knock out roughly half of Saudi oil production capacity.
Failed OPEC+ talks lead Saudi Arabia to slash prices and raise output, crashing prices.
Pandemic lockdowns collapse oil demand; US WTI briefly trades negative.
Russia's invasion triggers sanctions and supply fears, sending Brent prices sharply higher.
